subject: biology
title: 21M.1.HL.TZ1.32
question_html:
Which process does not take place in the stroma of chloroplasts?

A. Synthesis of carbohydrates

B. Fixation of carbon

C. Reduction of NADP

D. Synthesis of ribulose bisphosphate
examiners_html: Answers were divided almost equally between the four possible answers in this question suggesting a lot of guesswork but the discrimination index shows that better-prepared candidates tended to answer correctly. Arguably none of the answers is completely correct. Shrewd candidates could eliminate answers A and D, because if D, was correct, A was also, and both could not be correct. Reduction of NADP was the expected answer but arguably it occurs in the stroma, as the reaction takes place adjacent to the outer surface of the thylakoid membrane. Even so, this was the best answer.
